items.summary: Direct regional express (RE1) or a faster ICE, straight into Essen Hbf.
items.duration_hint: ~30-60 min direct, depending on the service

items.summary: Frequent direct ICE service (around 20 a day) straight into Essen Hbf.
Frequent direct ICE service straight into Essen Hbf.
Frequent direct ICE service straight into Essen Hbf.
One direct NS International ICE a day; otherwise change in Cologne or Duisburg.
Occasional direct ICE through to Essen; otherwise change onto a regional/ICE service in Cologne, which has frequent onward connections.
Some Thalys/ICE services run through to Essen without a change; others require changing onto a regional/ICE service in Cologne.
items.duration_hint: ~5h-5h30, sometimes with a change in Cologne

items.title: Düsseldorf Airport (DUS)
items.summary: The primary airport for the festival, with the region's widest range of international connections. The airport has its own train station.
items.duration_hint: ~25-30 min by direct regional train to Essen Hbf

items.title: Cologne Bonn Airport (CGN)
items.summary: Secondary airport further south, worth checking for extra carriers or cheaper fares. It also has its own train station at the terminal.
items.duration_hint: ~15 min by S-Bahn to Cologne Hbf, then change for Essen

items.title: Dortmund Airport (DTM)
items.summary: Smaller secondary airport, mainly budget carriers. No direct train at the airport itself.
items.duration_hint: Shuttle bus to Dortmund Hbf, then change for Essen

items.title: UNESCO Welterbe Zollverein
items.summary: Gelsenkirchener Strasse 181, 45309 Essen, Germany — use this address for GPS/navigation.
items.notes: Free parking at lots A1, A2 and B2 near the Coal Mine site; the paid parking deck at the Coking Plant (Im Welterbe 11) is roughly EUR1.80/hour, EUR7.50/day.

items.title: FlixBus to Essen Hauptbahnhof
items.summary: Long-distance coach network reaching Essen Hbf from cities across Europe, usually the cheapest option if you don't mind a longer ride.
